# UC-18.3.18 ┬Ę BGP EVPN Route Withdrawal Rate and Flap Storms

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Fault, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch for stretches when switches pull back lots of routing entries together so sharply that everyday programs stumble without warning long before anyone notices a loose cable or a flaky plug.*

---

## Description

BGP EVPN fabrics propagate overlay reachability through RFC 7432 and RFC 8365 NLRI families spanning Type-1 Ethernet Auto-Discovery, Type-2 MAC/IP advertisements, Type-3 inclusive multicast Ethernet-tag routes, Type-4 Ethernet-segment selection, and Type-5 IP-prefix overlays. WithdrawalsŌĆönot merely advertisementsŌĆödrive how fast speakers retract paths from RIB, ripple updates toward remote peers, and shrink dataplane ECMP breadth when redundant next hops evaporate in one blast. When **withdrawn** UPDATE volume spikes per peer within a minute bucket, convergence stretches beyond steady MAC-learning timers: downstream TOR switches purge Hardware bridging rows, spine route-reflector mirrors fan withdrawals outward, and operators observe lengthened convergence tails measured peer-to-peer rather than solely hop-count estimates.

Large bursts strain controller-plane CPUs still honoring UPDATE pacing, reorder BGP scanner queues, and interact badly with telemetry collectors polling **evpn statistics** concurrentlyŌĆöcreating observer-effect lag where Splunk timestamps trail CLI counters briefly yet storm signatures persist across sequential buckets. Withdrawal storms differ materially from benign mobility churn because paired Type-2 advertisements plus withdrawals cancel statistically during host moves; storms skew toward unmatched withdrawal dominance sourced from one **peer_ip**, aligning with leaf reload fingerprints, Ethernet-segment flap narratives tying Type-1 plus Type-4 retractions together, or route-target policy edits masquerading as dataplane faults unless annotated explicitly.

## Value

Splunk velocity math exposes storm fingerprints earlyŌĆöminutes ahead of synthetic probes noticing ECMP imbalanceŌĆöby aligning normalized **`withdrawn`** counters per **`route_bucket`**, **`peer_asn`**, and **`local_host`**. Teams gain predictive cues when **`storm_ratio`** clears three-sigma style multiples against rolling **`baseline_avg`** slices per peer family: Type-5 storms exceeding fifty withdrawals per minute flag stretched-L3 churn isolating perimeter mistakes from accidental spine resets; Type-3 thresholds north of five per minute spotlight multicast replication rebuilds deserving correlation against operational toggles; paired **`type2_withdrawals_per_min`** alongside advertisement counters distinguishes mobility symmetry from asymmetric teardown indicative of remote **VTEP** departure.

Operational outcomes include prioritized escalation keyed by **`storm_flag`** taxonomy (**`type5_storm`**, **`esi_segment_burst`**, **`type2_sigma_burst`**), **`ECMP set shrinkage`** forecasting via **`ecmp_shrink_pct`** when collectors expose hash-path cardinality deltas, forensic grouping using **`sample_prefix`** clusters lifted from syslog phrases or BMP-derived **`prefix`** lists, and convergence-extension narratives anchored on sequential **`event_time`** stamps correlated against UC-18.3.4 overlay timelines plus UC-18.3.14 topology postureŌĆöclosing loops between control-plane churn and silent east-west degradation without asking operators to babysit vendor CLIs continuously.

## Implementation

Deploy Splunkbase **2846**, **4495**, **2847** parsers on Heavy Forwarders; stage scripted **`show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics`** polls per spine-leaf peer list every **60s** into **`bgp:evpn:withdraw_event`** with **`FIELDALIAS`** mapping **`wdr_recv_count`** deltas into **`wdr_count`**; normalize syslog **`withdraw_hit`** signatures across **`cisco:nxos:bgp`**, **`arista:eos:bgp_evpn`**, **`juniper:junos:bgp`**, **`sonic:show:bgp_evpn`**, **`cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn`**; publish macro **`evpn_withdraw_velocity_core`** referencing governance-approved index unions.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk TA footprint ŌĆö Deploy Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS/NX-OS (Splunkbase 2846), Splunk Add-on for Arista EOS (Splunkbase 4495), and Splunk Add-on for Juniper (Splunkbase 2847) on Heavy Forwarders plus parsing Indexers so `cisco:nxos:bgp`, `cisco:nxos:syslog`, `arista:eos:bgp_evpn`, `juniper:junos:bgp` honor syslog PRI fidelity.
- Scripted telemetry ŌĆö Provision modular inputs or Ansible batches issuing `show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics` per `peer_ip` on 60 second cadences across Nexus 9000, `show bgp evpn statistics` on Arista 7280, `show bgp neighbor` referencing `table evpn.0` on QFX5120, `vtysh -c "show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics json"` on SONiC Dell Z9332, `cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn` gRPC lanes on Mellanox SN3700-class switches.
- Aggregator discipline ŌĆö Normalize `bgp:evpn:withdraw_event` sourcetype emitting `local_host`, `peer_ip`, `peer_asn`, `route_type`, `prefix`, `wdr_count`, `event_time`, optional `route_target_changed`, `route_refresh_event`, `ecmp_paths_before`/`ecmp_paths_after` deltas sourced from BMP exports when licensed.
- Roles ŌĆö Grant `network_observer` indexes `dc_fabric`, `network_ops`, `cisco_dc`, `network`, `os` per governance naming.

### Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
(1) Cisco Nexus 9000 ŌĆö Ensure `BGP-5-WITHDRAW`, `BGP-5-WITHDRAW_BATCH`, `BGP-3-RT_FILTER_ERROR`, `BGP-5-ADJCHANGE` mnemonics land `cisco:nxos:bgp` while `BGP-5-WITHDRAW` strings inside `cisco:nxos:syslog` remain searchableŌĆöstamp `FIELDALIAS` maps bridging `wdr_recv_count` counters parsed from `show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics neighbor` transcripts into `wdr_count` increments minute-over-minute.
(2) Arista EOS ŌĆö Capture `BGP-5-NOTIFICATION-RECEIVED` and `BGP-3-PREFIX-CHANGE` narratives inside `arista:eos:bgp_evpn`, aligning `withdraw_hit` regex families with `withdrawn` verbs emitted via `show bgp evpn statistics` JSON dumps duplicated through HEC when `eAPI` quotas permit.
(3) Juniper Junos ŌĆö Combine `RPD_BGP_NEIGHBOR_STATE_CHANGED` plus `RPD_PREFIX_THRESHOLD_EXCEEDED` syslog cues inside `juniper:junos:bgp` alongside scripted `received-routes` summaries referencing `evpn.0` tablesŌĆöpreserve `route_bucket` fidelity between syslog storms versus polled `withdrawn` counters.
(4) SONiC / FRRouting ŌĆö Serialize `sonic:show:bgp_evpn` JSON snapshots capturing `withdrawn` tallies without stripping `peer_ip` masksŌĆöconfirm `syncd` identities align `host` fields expected downstream.
(5) Cumulus NetQ ŌĆö Subscribe `cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn` feeds verifying ASN `peer_asn` parity versus CMDB `expected_bgp_peers` lookups borrowed from UC-18.3.14 guardrails.
(6) Custom aggregator ŌĆö Fuse scripted deltas plus syslog hits into `bgp:evpn:withdraw_event` so `storm_flag` SPL consumes unified `withdrawn_volume` math irrespective of ingestion laneŌĆöembed `suppress` macros honoring `firmware_upgrade_window`, `gr_helper_marker_walkthrough`, `mso_route_target_changeover` toggles emitted through lookups.

### Step 2 ŌĆö Splunk SPL search and alert configuration
Install saved search `evpn_withdraw_velocity_core` referencing the fenced SPL below with five-minute cron **`*/5` plus `earliest=-4h@m` investigative defaultsŌĆöwrap `route_bucket` thresholds inside macros `withdraw_threshold_type5`, `withdraw_threshold_type3`, `withdraw_sigma_multiplier` defaulting multiplier `3` aligning `baseline_avg` bursts.
Throttle paging `peer_ip` plus `route_bucket` pairs for `30` minutes unless `withdrawn_volume` doublesŌĆöprevent duplicate `storm_flag` tickets whenever `streamstats` windows overlap transient syslog bursts.
Annotate alerts with `sample_prefix` tails plus `peer_asn_seen` enabling downstream traceroute drills toward offending `VTEP`** attachments.
Escalate **`type5_storm`** rows faster than **`type2_sigma_burst`** because **`Type-5`** volumes remain comparatively tinyŌĆöprotect **`imet_multicast_tree_rebuild`** contexts via **`MaintenanceWindow`** overlays referencing **`CHANGE`** identifiers.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=os OR index=network)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:bgp" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:bgp" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="bgp:evpn:withdraw_event")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval local_host=trim(coalesce(local_host, hostname, switch_name, device_name, host, ""))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip, neighbor_ip, bgp_neighbor, neighbor_address, peer_address, remote_peer, ""))
| eval peer_asn=trim(coalesce(peer_asn, remote_as, neighbor_asn, asn_remote, ""))
| eval route_type=trim(coalesce(route_type, evpn_nlri_type, nlri_route_type, bgp_evpn_rt, ""))
| eval event_time=coalesce(event_time, _time)
| eval wdr_count=tonumber(coalesce(wdr_count, withdrawn_updates, withdraw_batch_count, withdraw_events, prefix_withdraw_cnt, wdr_recv_delta, ""))
| eval rt_upper=upper(route_type)
| eval route_bucket=case(
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?1|^1$|EAD|AUTO.?DISCOVERY"), "type1_ead",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?2|^2$|MAC.?IP"), "type2_mac_ip",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?3|^3$|IMET|INCLUSIVE"), "type3_imet",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?4|^4$|ESI|SEGMENT"), "type4_esi",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?5|^5$|PREFIX|IP.?PREFIX"), "type5_prefix",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)BGP-5-WITHDRAW|WITHDRAW_BATCH|wdr_recv"), "withdraw_sig_only",
    "type_unspecified")
| eval withdraw_hit=if(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)withdraw|withdrawn|wdr_recv|revoked") OR (isnotnull(wdr_count) AND wdr_count>0) OR lower(update_action)="withdraw",
    1, 0)
| where withdraw_hit=1
| eval vol=if(isnotnull(wdr_count) AND wdr_count>0, wdr_count, 1)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(vol) AS withdrawn_volume,
        latest(peer_asn) AS peer_asn_seen,
        values(prefix) AS sample_prefix,
        dc(route_bucket) AS route_family_mix
    BY _time local_host peer_ip route_bucket
| eval type2_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip", withdrawn_volume, null())
| eval type5_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type5_prefix", withdrawn_volume, null())
| eval type3_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type3_imet", withdrawn_volume, null())
| streamstats window=30 global=f avg(withdrawn_volume) AS baseline_avg BY local_host peer_ip route_bucket
| eval storm_ratio=if(baseline_avg>0 AND withdrawn_volume>(baseline_avg*3), withdrawn_volume/baseline_avg, null())
| eval storm_flag=case(
    route_bucket=="type5_prefix" AND withdrawn_volume>50, "type5_storm",
    route_bucket=="type3_imet" AND withdrawn_volume>5, "type3_anomaly",
    route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip" AND withdrawn_volume>1000 AND isnotnull(storm_ratio), "type2_velocity_burst",
    route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip" AND withdrawn_volume>100 AND withdrawn_volume>(baseline_avg*3), "type2_sigma_burst",
    route_bucket IN ("type4_esi","type1_ead") AND withdrawn_volume>100, "esi_segment_burst",
    null())
| where isnotnull(storm_flag)
| eval ecmp_paths_before=tonumber(ecmp_paths_before)
| eval ecmp_paths_after=tonumber(ecmp_paths_after)
| eval ecmp_shrink_pct=if(isnotnull(ecmp_paths_before) AND ecmp_paths_before>0 AND isnotnull(ecmp_paths_after), round(100*(ecmp_paths_before-ecmp_paths_after)/ecmp_paths_before, 2), null())
| sort - withdrawn_volume
| head 400
| table _time event_time local_host peer_ip peer_asn_seen route_bucket withdrawn_volume baseline_avg storm_ratio storm_flag type2_withdrawals_per_min type5_withdrawals_per_min type3_withdrawals_per_min route_family_mix sample_prefix ecmp_shrink_pct
```

Correlation narrative ŌĆö **`storm_ratio`** exposes **`withdrawn_volume`** multiples exceeding **`baseline_avg`** smoothingŌĆö**`route_bucket`** thresholds differentiate **`type5_storm`** severity versus **`type2_sigma_burst`** churn.

### Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
SSH parity drills comparing Splunk **`withdrawn_volume`** peaks versus **`show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics`** (**`wdr_recv_count`**/**`wdr_sent_count`**) counters on Nexus 9332C leavesŌĆöEOS **`show bgp evpn statistics`** mirrors **`peer_ip`** aggregatesŌĆöJunos **`show bgp neighbor`** **`received-routes`** referencing **`table evpn.0`** validates **`route_bucket`** taggingŌĆöSONiC **`vtysh`** JSON snapshots reconcile **`sonic:show:bgp_evpn`** ingest cadence versus syslog jitter.
vSphere CMDB overlays tie **`peer_ip`** storms against clustered host migrations explaining benign symmetryŌĆöreject **`storm_flag`** positives lacking CLI corroboration across **`baseline_avg`** recomputation whenever collectors stall.
Cross-view **`cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn`** dashboards purely directionalŌĆöSplunk macros remain authoritative when deltas diverge modestly.

### Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Dashboard Studio workspace **`BGP EVPN Withdrawal Velocity`** stacks heat-map **`peer_ip`** ├Ś **`route_bucket`** colored by **`withdrawn_volume`**ŌĆötimeline **`type2_withdrawals_per_min`** versus **`type5_withdrawals_per_min`** highlights **`storm_flag`** taxonomyŌĆögauge lane renders **`ecmp_shrink_pct`** alongside textual **`ECMP set shrinkage`** cues sourced when **`ecmp_paths_before`** telemetry exists.
Alert routing directs **`esi_segment_burst`** storms toward Ethernet-segment SMEsŌĆö**`type5_storm`** rows toward WAN-facing architectsŌĆö**`type3_anomaly`** toward multicast reviewers referencing **`imet_multicast_tree_rebuild`** overlays.
Embed Cisco **NDI** anomaly ribbons intersecting **`storm_ratio`** timelines via **`join`** **`splunk_server`** lookups referencing **`cisco:ndi:*`** feedsŌĆötie pager narratives mentioning **`withdrawn`** bursts explicitly versus **`graceful_restart`** helper contexts flagged **`suppress`** macros.

### Step 5 ŌĆö Tuning and extensions
Cross-correlate prolonged **`withdrawn_volume`** tails against UC-18.3.4 **`VXLAN`** overlay narratives distinguishing dataplane **`VTEP`** loss storms versus pure withdrawal churnŌĆönever substitute **`overlay_fault`** macros blindly without verifying **`withdraw_hit`** causality.
Suppress **`storm_flag`** outputs referencing **`firmware_upgrade_window`** CMDB tags paired with **`ISSU`** sequencing plus **`MaintenanceWindow`** lookups guarding **`gr_helper_marker_walkthrough`** intervals honoring **`STALE`** retention timers documented inside BGP policies.
Feed **`mso_route_target_changeover`** **`CHANGE`** identifiers through **`route_target_changed`** booleans preventing **`type5_storm`** escalations tied purely to **`route-target`** filter choreography lacking dataplane symptoms.
Augment forensic drills joining UC-18.3.14 **`expected_bgp_peers`** lookups verifying **`peer_asn_seen`** aligns design **`ASN`** tuples whenever **`esi_segment_burst`** suspicion emerges alongside **`LLDP`** posture anomalies absent elsewhere.
Integrate Cisco **NDI** anomaly exports highlighting BGP-EVPN convergence categoriesŌĆöblend anomaly timestamps with **`withdrawn_volume`** spikes reinforcing pager narratives referencing **`fabric-control-plane-stability`** posture reviews quarterly.
Tune **`streamstats`** **`window`** sizes per fabric scaleŌĆödense multitenant estates may lengthen **`baseline_avg`** smoothing horizons preventing **`type2_sigma_burst`** chatter triggered nightly **`ARP`** rebalance chores unrelated materially toward faults elsewhere noted faithfully.
- **Silent alerts despite syslog spikes** ŌĆö widen **`withdraw_hit`** regex families capturing **`BGP-5-WITHDRAW_BATCH`** multi-line syslog blobsŌĆöconfirm **`props.conf`** **`LINE_BREAKER`** preserves **`WITHDRAW`** lines spanning newline boundaries.
- **`baseline_avg` stalls near zero** ŌĆö scripted **`bgp:evpn:withdraw_event`** collectors pausedŌĆöinspect **`splunkd.log`** **`ExecProcessor`** return codes alongside Ansible **`TASK`** banners referencing unreachable **`mgmt`** loops.
- **`ecmp_shrink_pct` always null** ŌĆö collectors omit **`ecmp_paths_before`** fieldsŌĆöextend **`show`** transcripts harvesting **`nexthop`** breadth counts aligned per **`route`** hash groups documented vendor-side.
- **`storm_ratio` perpetual spikes** ŌĆö **`window`** too narrow versus **`bin`** **`span=1m`** cadenceŌĆöexpand **`streamstats`** **`window`** toward **`45`** buckets smoothing intermittent bursts honoring **`withdraw_sigma_multiplier`** reviewer approvals quarterly archived faithfully.

## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=os OR index=network)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:bgp" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:bgp" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="bgp:evpn:withdraw_event")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval local_host=trim(coalesce(local_host, hostname, switch_name, device_name, host, ""))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip, neighbor_ip, bgp_neighbor, neighbor_address, peer_address, remote_peer, ""))
| eval peer_asn=trim(coalesce(peer_asn, remote_as, neighbor_asn, asn_remote, ""))
| eval route_type=trim(coalesce(route_type, evpn_nlri_type, nlri_route_type, bgp_evpn_rt, ""))
| eval event_time=coalesce(event_time, _time)
| eval wdr_count=tonumber(coalesce(wdr_count, withdrawn_updates, withdraw_batch_count, withdraw_events, prefix_withdraw_cnt, wdr_recv_delta, ""))
| eval rt_upper=upper(route_type)
| eval route_bucket=case(
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?1|^1$|EAD|AUTO.?DISCOVERY"), "type1_ead",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?2|^2$|MAC.?IP"), "type2_mac_ip",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?3|^3$|IMET|INCLUSIVE"), "type3_imet",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?4|^4$|ESI|SEGMENT"), "type4_esi",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?5|^5$|PREFIX|IP.?PREFIX"), "type5_prefix",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)BGP-5-WITHDRAW|WITHDRAW_BATCH|wdr_recv"), "withdraw_sig_only",
    "type_unspecified")
| eval withdraw_hit=if(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)withdraw|withdrawn|wdr_recv|revoked") OR (isnotnull(wdr_count) AND wdr_count>0) OR lower(update_action)="withdraw",
    1, 0)
| where withdraw_hit=1
| eval vol=if(isnotnull(wdr_count) AND wdr_count>0, wdr_count, 1)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(vol) AS withdrawn_volume,
        latest(peer_asn) AS peer_asn_seen,
        values(prefix) AS sample_prefix,
    BY _time local_host peer_ip route_bucket
| eval type2_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip", withdrawn_volume, null())
| eval type5_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type5_prefix", withdrawn_volume, null())
| eval type3_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type3_imet", withdrawn_volume, null())

| streamstats window=30 global=f avg(withdrawn_volume) AS baseline_avg BY local_host peer_ip route_bucket
| eval storm_ratio=if(baseline_avg>0 AND withdrawn_volume>(baseline_avg*3), withdrawn_volume/baseline_avg, null())
| eval storm_flag=case(
    route_bucket=="type5_prefix" AND withdrawn_volume>50, "type5_storm",
    route_bucket=="type3_imet" AND withdrawn_volume>5, "type3_anomaly",
    route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip" AND withdrawn_volume>1000 AND isnotnull(storm_ratio), "type2_velocity_burst",
    route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip" AND withdrawn_volume>100 AND withdrawn_volume>(baseline_avg*3), "type2_sigma_burst",
    route_bucket IN ("type4_esi","type1_ead") AND withdrawn_volume>100, "esi_segment_burst",
    null())
| where isnotnull(storm_flag)
| eval ecmp_paths_before=tonumber(ecmp_paths_before)
| eval ecmp_paths_after=tonumber(ecmp_paths_after)
| eval ecmp_shrink_pct=if(isnotnull(ecmp_paths_before) AND ecmp_paths_before>0 AND isnotnull(ecmp_paths_after), round(100*(ecmp_paths_before-ecmp_paths_after)/ecmp_paths_before, 2), null())
| sort - withdrawn_volume
| head 400
| table _time event_time local_host peer_ip peer_asn_seen route_bucket withdrawn_volume baseline_avg storm_ratio storm_flag type2_withdrawals_per_min type5_withdrawals_per_min type3_withdrawals_per_min sample_prefix ecmp_shrink_pct
```

## Visualization

(1) **Heat-map** ŌĆö **`peer_ip`** ├Ś **`route_bucket`** colored by **`withdrawn_volume`** per minute. (2) **Timechart** ŌĆö **`type2_withdrawals_per_min`** stacked alongside **`type5_withdrawals_per_min`**. (3) **Gauge row** ŌĆö **`ecmp_shrink_pct`** worst offenders with **`storm_flag`** overlays. (4) **Table** ŌĆö top storms listing **`local_host`**, **`peer_asn_seen`**, **`storm_ratio`**, **`sample_prefix`**. (5) **Timeline** ŌĆö sequential **`event_time`** markers paired with Cisco **NDI** anomaly ribbons when **`cisco:ndi:*`** lanes exist.

## Known False Positives

**firmware_upgrade_window** ŌĆö Orchestrated leaf reloads during supervised firmware pushes deliberately tear down BGP-EVPN tables for roughly thirty-to-sixty seconds while images commitŌĆöwithdrawal counters leap upward yet operators already expect that choreographyŌĆösuppress **`storm_flag`** hits whenever CMDB tags **`firmware_upgrade_window`** pair **`local_host`** identities with CAB-approved **`ISSU`** sequencing backed by reload tickets tied to those hosts.

**gr_helper_marker_walkthrough** ŌĆö Peers honoring **`RFC`** **`4724`** **`Restart`** **`helper`** semantics retain **`STALE`** markings while **`WITHDRAWN`** tallies oscillate apart from instantaneous dataplane outagesŌĆöcross-check syslog **`Neighbor`** banners citing **`Graceful`** timers alongside **`Established`** uptime versus **`withdraw_hit`** timelines before blaming **`esi_segment_burst`** fingerprints alone.

**mso_route_target_changeover** ŌĆö Multi-site orchestrator pushes sometimes revise **`route-target`** import/export tuplesŌĆöroutes briefly **`withdrawn`** then readvertised resembling stormsŌĆöhonor **`route_target_changed`** booleans inside **`bgp:evpn:withdraw_event`** aggregates plus **`BGP-3-RT_FILTER_ERROR`** narratives before escalating **`type5_storm`** outcomes tied to unrelated WAN faults.

**imet_multicast_tree_rebuild** ŌĆö Operators flipping **BUM** replication (**HEAD**, **MTEP**, **SOURCE**) modes or adjusting overlay **MTU** knobs rebuild inclusive multicast Ethernet-tag treesŌĆö**Type-3** withdrawals spike benignlyŌĆötie positives to **`MaintenanceWindow`** tickets citing **`imet_multicast_tree_rebuild`** chores rather than paging multicast cores absent corroborating optics alarms.

**route_refresh_event** ŌĆö Outbound **`route-map`** edits routinely solicit **`route-refresh`** exchangesŌĆöboth peers **`withdraw`** then re-announce prefixes symmetricallyŌĆöcarry **`route_refresh_event`** markers harvested from syslog (**`BGP-5-ROUTEREFRESH`** style signatures) alongside **`CHG`** identifiers describing policy edits so **`withdrawn_volume`** surges downgrade automatically.

**mac_aging_time_reconfig** ŌĆö Operators shortening MAC aging timers provoke mass **`Type-2`** withdrawals followed by fresh advertisements while tables stabilizeŌĆöannotate **`mac_aging_time_reconfig`** maintenance tickets suppressing pager noise distinct from stealth evacuations lacking paired ticketing clues.

**evpn_arp_routing_toggle** ŌĆö Feature toggles enabling or disabling **`evpn-arp-routing`** reshape host-route advertisement behaviorŌĆölarge one-time **`withdraw`** bursts precede steady counters afterwardŌĆösuppress until **`baseline_avg`** recomputes post-change following automation logs documenting **`evpn_arp_routing_toggle`** intent.

## References

- [IETF RFC 7432 ŌĆö BGP MPLS-Based Ethernet VPN](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7432)
- [IETF RFC 8365 ŌĆö Network Virtualization Overlay Solution Using BGP EVPN](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8365)
- [Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS BGP EVPN Configuration Guide](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/nx-os/nexus9000/103x/configuration/evpn/)
- [Splunk Lantern ŌĆö practice guidance library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
